claim: Algarve summer winds average 5–10 knots with occasional 15-knot gusts near Ponta da Piedade, Lagos; within 1 nautical mile of the Algarve coast, winds average 7 knots.
stance_target: Quantified Algarve wind speed for scent plume dispersion modeling
scope_conditions: Algarve coast, Portugal; summer season; from yachting operational data
quoted_support: Winds here average 5-10 knots in summer, with occasional 15-knot gusts near Ponta da Piedade in Lagos... winds are lighter within 1 nautical mile of the coast, averaging 7 knots
numbers: 7 knots average within 1 nm of coast

claim: Algarve sea breezes strengthen to 12 knots in the afternoon as land heats up — morning conditions (before 2 PM) are calmer at 5–10 knots, then wind peaks in the afternoon.
stance_target: Search timing recommendation: SAR dog searches and scent detection should be conducted in early morning before Nortada/sea breeze strengthening
scope_conditions: Algarve coastal sailing; daytime thermal wind cycle
quoted_support: monitor sea breezes—they strengthen to 12 knots in the afternoon as land heats up, so plan longer sails before 2 PM

claim: The Nortada in Cascais/northern Portugal reaches 15–20 knots in the afternoon with gusts up to 30 knots, while the Algarve experiences a lighter, more sheltered version of the same northerly wind pattern.
stance_target: Calibrating scent plume dispersion intensity for Algarve vs. northern Portuguese coast
scope_conditions: Portuguese coast; summer Nortada; Cascais vs. Algarve regional comparison
quoted_support: strong westerly winds, averaging 10-20 knots in summer, with gusts up to 30 knots during the Nortada in Cascais... the Algarve's flat coastline allows for more consistent breezes, averaging 7 knots near Lagos

claim: The Levante (east wind) can push temperatures past 40°C in the Algarve with no sea breeze for relief — creating extreme dehydration risk windows distinct from the Nortada northerly pattern.
stance_target: Levante episodes as extreme dehydration accelerator for lost dogs, warranting emergency water station activation
scope_conditions: Algarve; episodic Levante wind events, distinct from Nortada
quoted_support: short spells of east wind called Levante can push temperatures past 40°C with no sea breeze for relief
